CVE-2016-9797.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer over-read was observed in "l2cap_dump" fu ...
CVE-2016-9797.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer over-read was observed in "l2cap_dump" function in "tools/parser/l2cap.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in hcidump crash.

CVE-2016-9798.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a use-after-free was identified in "conf_opt" func ...
CVE-2016-9798.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a use-after-free was identified in "conf_opt" function in "tools/parser/l2cap.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in hcidump crash.

CVE-2016-9799.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "pklg_read_hci"  ...
CVE-2016-9799.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "pklg_read_hci" function in "btsnoop.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in btmon crash.

CVE-2016-9800.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "pin_code_reply_ ...
CVE-2016-9800.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "pin_code_reply_dump" function in "tools/parser/hci.c" source file. The issue exists because "pin" array is overflowed by supplied parameter due to lack of boundary checks on size of the buffer from frame "pin_code_reply_cp *cp" parameter.

CVE-2016-9801.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "set_ext_ctrl" f ...
CVE-2016-9801.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "set_ext_ctrl" function in "tools/parser/l2cap.c" source file when processing corrupted dump file.

CVE-2016-9802.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer over-read was identified in "l2cap_packet ...
CVE-2016-9802.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer over-read was identified in "l2cap_packet" function in "monitor/packet.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in btmon crash.

CVE-2016-9803.title: In BlueZ 5.42, an out-of-bounds read was observed in "le_meta_ev_ ...
CVE-2016-9803.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, an out-of-bounds read was observed in "le_meta_ev_dump" function in "tools/parser/hci.c" source file. This issue exists because 'subevent' (which is used to read correct element from 'ev_le_meta_str' array) is overflowed.

CVE-2016-9804.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "commands_dump"  ...
CVE-2016-9804.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "commands_dump" function in "tools/parser/csr.c" source file. The issue exists because "commands" array is overflowed by supplied parameter due to lack of boundary checks on size of the buffer from frame "frm->ptr" parameter. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in hcidump crash.

CVE-2016-9917.title: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "read_n" functio ...
CVE-2016-9917.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, a buffer overflow was observed in "read_n" function in "tools/hcidump.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in hcidump crash.

CVE-2016-9918.title: In BlueZ 5.42, an out-of-bounds read was identified in "packet_he ...
CVE-2016-9918.summary: In BlueZ 5.42, an out-of-bounds read was identified in "packet_hexdump" function in "monitor/packet.c" source file. This issue can be triggered by processing a corrupted dump file and will result in btmon crash.

CVE-2020-10134.title: Pairing in Bluetooth® Core v5.2 and earlier may permit an unauthe ...
CVE-2020-10134.summary: Pairing in Bluetooth® Core v5.2 and earlier may permit an unauthenticated attacker to acquire credentials with two pairing devices via adjacent access when the unauthenticated user initiates different pairing methods in each peer device and an end-user erroneously completes both pairing procedures with the MITM using the confirmation number of one peer as the passkey of the other. An adjacent, unauthenticated attacker could be able to initiate any Bluetooth operation on either attacked device exposed by the enabled Bluetooth profiles. This exposure may be limited when the user must authorize certain access explicitly, but so long as a user assumes that it is the intended remote device requesting permissions, device-local protections may be weakened.

CVE-2020-26558.title: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR secure pairing in Bluetooth Core Specific ...
CVE-2020-26558.summary: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR secure pairing in Bluetooth Core Specification 2.1 through 5.2 may permit a nearby man-in-the-middle attacker to identify the Passkey used during pairing (in the Passkey authentication procedure) by reflection of the public key and the authentication evidence of the initiating device, potentially permitting this attacker to complete authenticated pairing with the responding device using the correct Passkey for the pairing session. The attack methodology determines the Passkey value one bit at a time.

CVE-2020-27153.title: In BlueZ before 5.55, a double free was found in the gatttool dis ...
CVE-2020-27153.summary: In BlueZ before 5.55, a double free was found in the gatttool disconnect_cb() routine from shared/att.c. A remote attacker could potentially cause a denial of service or code execution, during service discovery, due to a redundant disconnect MGMT event.

CVE-2020-9770.title: A logic issue was addressed with improved state management. This  ...
CVE-2020-9770.summary: A logic issue was addressed with improved state management. This issue is fixed in iOS 13.4 and iPadOS 13.4. An attacker in a privileged network position may be able to intercept Bluetooth traffic.

CVE-2021-0129.title: Improper access control in BlueZ may allow an authenticated user  ...
CVE-2021-0129.summary: Improper access control in BlueZ may allow an authenticated user to potentially enable information disclosure via adjacent access.

CVE-2021-3588.title: The cli_feat_read_cb() function in src/gatt-database.c does not p ...
CVE-2021-3588.summary: The cli_feat_read_cb() function in src/gatt-database.c does not perform bounds checks on the 'offset' variable before using it as an index into an array for reading.

CVE-2021-3658.title: bluetoothd from bluez incorrectly saves adapters' Discoverable st ...
CVE-2021-3658.summary: bluetoothd from bluez incorrectly saves adapters' Discoverable status when a device is powered down, and restores it when powered up. If a device is powered down while discoverable, it will be discoverable when powered on again. This could lead to inadvertent exposure of the bluetooth stack to physically nearby attackers.

CVE-2021-37577.title: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR Secure Connections pairing and Secure Sim ...
CVE-2021-37577.summary: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR Secure Connections pairing and Secure Simple Pairing using the Passkey entry protocol in Bluetooth Core Specifications 2.1 through 5.3 may permit an unauthenticated man-in-the-middle attacker to identify the Passkey used during pairing by reflection of a crafted public key with the same X coordinate as the offered public key and by reflection of the authentication evidence of the initiating device, potentially permitting this attacker to complete authenticated pairing with the responding device using the correct Passkey for the pairing session. This is a related issue to CVE-2020-26558.

CVE-2021-41229.title: BlueZ is a Bluetooth protocol stack for Linux. In affected versio ...
CVE-2021-41229.summary: BlueZ is a Bluetooth protocol stack for Linux. In affected versions a vulnerability exists in sdp_cstate_alloc_buf which allocates memory which will always be hung in the singly linked list of cstates and will not be freed. This will cause a memory leak over time. The data can be a very large object, which can be caused by an attacker continuously sending sdp packets and this may cause the service of the target device to crash.

CVE-2021-43400.title: An issue was discovered in gatt-database.c in BlueZ 5.61. A use-a ...
CVE-2021-43400.summary: An issue was discovered in gatt-database.c in BlueZ 5.61. A use-after-free can occur when a client disconnects during D-Bus processing of a WriteValue call.

CVE-2022-0204.title: A heap overflow vulnerability was found in bluez in versions prio ...
CVE-2022-0204.summary: A heap overflow vulnerability was found in bluez in versions prior to 5.63. An attacker with local network access could pass specially crafted files causing an application to halt or crash, leading to a denial of service.

CVE-2022-39176.title: BlueZ before 5.59 allows physically proximate attackers to obtain ...
CVE-2022-39176.summary: BlueZ before 5.59 allows physically proximate attackers to obtain sensitive information because profiles/audio/avrcp.c does not validate params_len.

CVE-2022-39177.title: BlueZ before 5.59 allows physically proximate attackers to cause  ...
CVE-2022-39177.summary: BlueZ before 5.59 allows physically proximate attackers to cause a denial of service because malformed and invalid capabilities can be processed in profiles/audio/avdtp.c.

CVE-2023-27349.title: BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Improper Validation of Array Index Remo ...
CVE-2023-27349.summary:
BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Improper Validation of Array Index Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows network-adjacent attackers to execute arbitrary code via Bluetooth on affected installations of BlueZ. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must connect to a malicious device.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of the AVRCP protocol. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of user-supplied data, which can result in a write past the end of an allocated buffer. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of root. Was ZDI-CAN-19908.

CVE-2023-44431.title: BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Stack-based Buffer Overflow Remote Code ...
CVE-2023-44431.summary:
BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Stack-based Buffer Overflow Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows network-adjacent attackers to execute arbitrary code via Bluetooth on affected installations of BlueZ. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must connect to a malicious device.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of the AVRCP protocol. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of the length of user-supplied data prior to copying it to a fixed-length stack-based buffer. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of root. Was ZDI-CAN-19909.

CVE-2023-45866.title: Bluetooth HID Hosts in BlueZ may permit an unauthenticated Periph ...
CVE-2023-45866.summary: Bluetooth HID Hosts in BlueZ may permit an unauthenticated Peripheral role HID Device to initiate and establish an encrypted connection, and accept HID keyboard reports, potentially permitting injection of HID messages when no user interaction has occurred in the Central role to authorize such access. An example affected package is bluez 5.64-0ubuntu1 in Ubuntu 22.04LTS. NOTE: in some cases, a CVE-2020-0556 mitigation would have already addressed this Bluetooth HID Hosts issue.

CVE-2023-50229.title: BlueZ Phone Book Access Profile Heap-based Buffer Overflow Remote ...

User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must connect to a malicious Bluetooth device.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of the Phone Book Access profile. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of the length of user-supplied data prior to copying it to a fixed-length heap-based buffer. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of root. Was ZDI-CAN-20936.

CVE-2022-3563.title: A vulnerability classified as problematic has been found in Linux ...
CVE-2022-3563.summary: A vulnerability classified as problematic has been found in Linux Kernel. Affected is the function read_50_controller_cap_complete of the file tools/mgmt-tester.c of the component BlueZ. The manipulation of the argument cap_len leads to null pointer dereference. It is recommended to apply a patch to fix this issue. VDB-211086 is the identifier assigned to this vulnerability.

CVE-2023-51596.title: BlueZ Phone Book Access Profile Heap-based Buffer Overflow Remote ...
CVE-2023-51596.summary:
BlueZ Phone Book Access Profile Heap-based Buffer Overflow Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows network-adjacent attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of BlueZ. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must connect to a malicious Bluetooth device.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of the Phone Book Access profile. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of the length of user-supplied data prior to copying it to a fixed-length heap-based buffer. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of root. Was ZDI-CAN-20939.

CVE-2020-10134.title: Pairing in Bluetooth® Core v5.2 and earlier may permit an unauthe ...
CVE-2020-10134.summary: Pairing in Bluetooth® Core v5.2 and earlier may permit an unauthenticated attacker to acquire credentials with two pairing devices via adjacent access when the unauthenticated user initiates different pairing methods in each peer device and an end-user erroneously completes both pairing procedures with the MITM using the confirmation number of one peer as the passkey of the other. An adjacent, unauthenticated attacker could be able to initiate any Bluetooth operation on either attacked device exposed by the enabled Bluetooth profiles. This exposure may be limited when the user must authorize certain access explicitly, but so long as a user assumes that it is the intended remote device requesting permissions, device-local protections may be weakened.

CVE-2020-9770.title: A logic issue was addressed with improved state management. This  ...
CVE-2020-9770.summary: A logic issue was addressed with improved state management. This issue is fixed in iOS 13.4 and iPadOS 13.4. An attacker in a privileged network position may be able to intercept Bluetooth traffic.

CVE-2021-37577.title: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR Secure Connections pairing and Secure Sim ...
CVE-2021-37577.summary: Bluetooth LE and BR/EDR Secure Connections pairing and Secure Simple Pairing using the Passkey entry protocol in Bluetooth Core Specifications 2.1 through 5.3 may permit an unauthenticated man-in-the-middle attacker to identify the Passkey used during pairing by reflection of a crafted public key with the same X coordinate as the offered public key and by reflection of the authentication evidence of the initiating device, potentially permitting this attacker to complete authenticated pairing with the responding device using the correct Passkey for the pairing session. This is a related issue to CVE-2020-26558.

CVE-2023-27349.title: BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Improper Validation of Array Index Remo ...
CVE-2023-27349.summary:
BlueZ Audio Profile AVRCP Improper Validation of Array Index Remote Code Execution Vulnerability. This vulnerability allows network-adjacent attackers to execute arbitrary code via Bluetooth on affected installations of BlueZ. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must connect to a malicious device.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of the AVRCP protocol. The issue results from the lack of proper validation of user-supplied data, which can result in a write past the end of an allocated buffer. An attacker can leverage this vulnerability to execute code in the context of root. Was ZDI-CAN-19908.

CVE-2023-45866.title: Bluetooth HID Hosts in BlueZ may permit an unauthenticated Periph ...
CVE-2023-45866.summary: Bluetooth HID Hosts in BlueZ may permit an unauthenticated Peripheral role HID Device to initiate and establish an encrypted connection, and accept HID keyboard reports, potentially permitting injection of HID messages when no user interaction has occurred in the Central role to authorize such access. An example affected package is bluez 5.64-0ubuntu1 in Ubuntu 22.04LTS. NOTE: in some cases, a CVE-2020-0556 mitigation would have already addressed this Bluetooth HID Hosts issue.
